content.text:
Fixed and merged ([PR #11](https://github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/pull/11), `e13619b`).

## Root cause (measured, not guessed)
Claude Code sessions are huge (thousands of lines vs Codex's ~50). I profiled a real 5014-line session: the condensed analysis input was **dominated by assistant message text — 610 messages / 142 KB, over half the input** — producing one ~100k-token prompt that a local model (gemma4) prompt-evals for minutes. It was a single chunk, not chunk-explosion, so the lever was input size, not chunking.

## Fix
Aligned the shared analyzer input with what **Antigravity** already does: keep user requests, agent tool calls, and failed outputs; **drop assistant narration** (plus successful outputs/reasoning). The summary is built from "what the agent did / what went wrong," so no real signal is lost — and all sources are now consistent.

## Result (gemma4/Ollama, that same 5014-line session)
- Analysis input **~250 KB → ~108 KB (~57% smaller)**
- Summarize: **didn't complete → ~2.5 min**, still a single chunk. Smaller/typical sessions are correspondingly quick.

Shipped via the workflow: branch → adapter tests updated (assert narration omitted) → verify (158 tests green) → PR #11 → CI green → squash-merge on green, branch pruned.

One honest caveat: the *remaining* time is inherent local-model prompt-eval on very large sessions (the 28k-line monster will still be multi-chunk and slow on an 8B local model). If you want big sessions summarized fast, pointing at **Gemini** (`AI_PROVIDER=gemini` + key) is the lever — cloud prompt-eval is far faster. Want me to do anything further there, or is this good?
Did we loose any fidelity?

Good question — and the answer splits cleanly by layer:

## What you *see* (the timeline): no fidelity lost
The change only touched the analyzer's input builder (`NormalizedSteps`), **not** the transcript adapter (`toSteps`/`toTranscriptJson`). So the rendered timeline is byte-for-byte the same as before — every assistant message, thinking block, tool call (full args), and tool output (success and error) still displays in full. Nothing was removed from what's visualized.

## What the *summarizer reads* (analysis input): yes, intentionally
The AI summary is generated from a condensed projection of the session, and that projection now **omits the assistant's prose narration**. After the change, the LLM sees:
- ✅ User requests (truncated 2000 chars)
- ✅ Agent tool calls (name + key arg, truncated ~200)
- ✅ Failed tool outputs (truncated 500)
- ❌ Assistant text/commentary — **newly dropped**
- ❌ Successful tool outputs, reasoning/thinking — *already* dropped before this change

So the concrete loss is: the model no longer reads the agent's between-step commentary or its final wrap-up message when writing the summary. If the agent stated a decision or conclusion *only in prose* (not reflected in any tool action), the summarizer won't see it directly — it re-derives the narrative from the requests, actions, and errors instead.

Two things worth knowing:
- This is exactly how the **Antigravity** analyzer has always worked (it never fed model message text to the summarizer), so it's the same fidelity bar the tool was built on — I made Codex/Claude Code consistent with it.
- The summary was always a lossy compression by design (truncation, omitted outputs); this widened that one category.

## If you want some of it back
There's a sensible middle ground that preserves outcome fidelity cheaply: include the assistant's **final** message (the wrap-up/outcome) and/or cap total assistant text to a small global budget, instead of either "all of it" (slow) or "none." That recovers the agent's stated conclusions without re-bloating the input.

Want me to add that — keep the last assistant message (and maybe a small budget for the rest)? It's a small, testable change if the current summaries feel like they're missing the agent's conclusions.
Yeah lets do that. I want to make sure we get the best summaries we can.
On it — the highest-value addition is the agent's **final** message (the outcome), plus a small budget for earlier narration. Let me update `NormalizedSteps`.

content.text:
Done — merged ([PR #12](https://github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/pull/12), `b077053`).

## What changed
To answer your earlier "did we lose fidelity?" — we had dropped *all* assistant narration from the summarizer's input. Now the analyzer keeps the part that matters most for summary quality:

- **The final assistant message** (the agent's outcome/conclusion) is always included (≤2000 chars).
- **Earlier assistant messages** get a small shared budget (2000 chars total, 500 each); the rest stay dropped.

So summaries regain "what was accomplished / what the agent concluded" — the most valuable narration — while the speed win holds. On a large Claude Code session this adds back only a few KB versus the ~142 KB of full prose I measured, so it stays a single fast chunk.

## Net fidelity picture now
- **Visualization**: unchanged — full transcript always rendered.
- **Summarizer input**: user requests + agent tool calls + failed outputs + **final outcome message** + a little early narration. Still omits mid-session prose, successful tool outputs, and reasoning (those were always omitted / are bulky and low-signal).

## Verification & workflow
- New `NormalizedStepsTest` locks the final-message-kept + budget behavior; adapter tests assert the final message is included.
- **160 tests** (97 backend + 44 frontend + 19 e2e), CI green on all three jobs → squash-merged on green, branch pruned, `main` synced.

If after using it you find summaries still missing something specific, the budget knobs (`ASSISTANT_FINAL_MAX` / `ASSISTANT_OTHER_BUDGET`) are easy to tune — tell me what feels thin and I'll adjust.
how do I run this with the gemini key again?

Gemini is the default provider, so you just provide the key and (if it's still set from the Ollama runs) clear `AI_PROVIDER`:

```bash
unset AI_PROVIDER          # only needed if you set it to "ollama" earlier this session
export GEMINI_API_KEY="your-key-here"
mise exec -- ./gradlew run
```

Then open http://localhost:8080.

Notes:
- `mise exec --` ensures it runs on JDK 25 (the project's pinned toolchain). If you have mise shell activation, plain `./gradlew run` works too.
- `AI_PROVIDER` defaults to `gemini` when unset, so you don't need to set it — just make sure it isn't still `ollama` in your shell.
- Optional: `export GEMINI_MODEL=...` to override the model (default `gemini-3.5-flash`), and `export MICRONAUT_SERVER_PORT=9090` for a different port.
- With Gemini, those large Claude Code sessions will summarize much faster than they did on the local model.

- This is an external-model builder setting (no getter), so it's not unit-assertable; `ChatModelFactoryTest` still confirms the model builds. Backend + frontend + e2e all green.

Two things here — your run question and the error:

## Running with the Gemini key
```bash
unset AI_PROVIDER          # if it's still "ollama" from earlier
export GEMINI_API_KEY="your-key-here"
mise exec -- ./gradlew run
```
→ http://localhost:8080. Gemini is the default provider, so that's all you need.

## The error (now fixed, `df4430a`)
That was a **transient Gemini API timeout** on the final consolidation call. The model was built with a 2-min timeout and **no retries**, so one slow response aborted the whole analysis *after* all the per-chunk work had already succeeded. I bumped it to **3-min timeout + 2 retries**, so transient HTTP/network hiccups get retried instead of failing the run.

- Shipped via the workflow: branch → verify (160 tests green) → PR #13 → CI green → squash-merged on green, branch pruned.
- Pull `main` and re-run — re-trigger the analysis (use the ✨ Recompute button to bypass any error that got cached). It should now ride through transient slowness.

If it ever times out *persistently* on a very large session (3 min × 3 attempts genuinely exceeded), that's a real size limit rather than a blip — the next lever is making consolidation failure **graceful** (return the partial per-chunk summary instead of erroring) and/or lowering the consolidation threshold so it sends smaller merge requests. You are a senior code reviewer. Review the STAGED git diff in /Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer (run `git diff --cached`). This adds graceful failure handling to AI analysis in `AnalysisController`.

Changes:
1. When the LLM consolidation (`recursivelyConsolidate`) fails, the controller now catches it and falls back to `localMerge(seqResponses)` — a deterministic, no-LLM merge of the per-chunk `AnalysisResponse`s — so the user gets a usable summary instead of "Error generating summary".
2. `recursivelyConsolidate` was refactored: token-estimation failure now falls back to a char-length heuristic (instead of a catch that also swallowed consolidation errors), a `responses.size() <= 1` guard was added, and consolidation exceptions now propagate to the caller (so the fallback fires).
3. Empty `seqResponses` now distinguishes "No transcript lines found." (no chunks) from "Analysis could not be generated..." (chunks existed but the model failed for all).
4. `localMerge` combines shortTitle/summary/flow/agentActions/issues/recommendations with dedup + caps, prefixing the summary with a "(Combined from N partial analyses...)" note.
5. Tests added: consolidation-failure → local merge; all-chunks-fail → clear message. Failure injected via mock flags.

Focus on:
1. **Correctness of the fallback control flow**: does a consolidation timeout now reliably reach `localMerge`? Any path where it still throws to the outer "Error generating summary" catch? Is the fallback result cached (so it isn't recomputed every load) — and is that desirable?
2. **Recursion safety**: the new `responses.size() <= 1` guard — does it prevent the previously-possible infinite recursion when a single response exceeds budget? Any case where recursion still doesn't terminate? Note: with consolidation now propagating exceptions, does a deep recursive split call consolidate at each level — could a mid-recursion failure leave partial state? (It's all caught at the top, so fine — but confirm.)
3. **localMerge correctness**: null-safety on record accessors, dedup/cap behavior, summary length cap, sensible defaults.
4. **Behavior preservation**: Antigravity/Codex/Claude-code happy paths unchanged; the multi-chunk success test still valid.
5. **Tests**: do they actually exercise the fallback (not pass trivially)? Gaps?
6. Bugs.

feat(ai): gracefully degrade analysis instead of erroring

When LLM consolidation fails (e.g. an API timeout on the final merge), the
analysis previously aborted with "Error generating summary", discarding all
the successful per-chunk work. Now it degrades gracefully:

- On consolidation failure, fall back to a deterministic local merge of the
  per-chunk analyses (no LLM) so the user still gets a usable summary,
  prefixed with a "(Combined from N partial analyses...)" note.
- The degraded fallback is NOT cached, so a later (non-forced) load retries
  the real LLM consolidation rather than serving the cruder summary.
- recursivelyConsolidate now lets consolidation errors propagate (so the
  fallback fires), uses a char-length heuristic only when token estimation
  fails, and guards responses.size() <= 1 to prevent a possible infinite
  recursion on a single oversized response.
- When the model fails for every chunk, return a clear "Analysis could not
  be generated... please try again" message instead of "No transcript lines
  found".

Tests: consolidation-failure -> local merge (and not cached); all-chunks-
fail -> clear message. 99 backend + 44 frontend + 19 e2e, all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

## Why

When LLM consolidation failed (e.g. the Gemini API timeout you hit on the final merge), the whole analysis aborted with "Error generating summary" — throwing away all the successful per-chunk work.

## What

- **Local-merge fallback**: on consolidation failure, deterministically merge the per-chunk `AnalysisResponse`s (no LLM) so the user still gets a usable summary, prefixed with `(Combined from N partial analyses; full consolidation was unavailable.)`.
- **Don't cache the degraded result**: the failure is usually transient, so a later (non-forced) load retries the real consolidation rather than serving the cruder summary.
- **`recursivelyConsolidate` hardening**: consolidation errors now propagate (so the fallback fires); a char-length heuristic is used only when token *estimation* fails; and a `responses.size() <= 1` guard prevents a possible infinite recursion on a single oversized response.
- **Clear all-failed message**: when the model fails for every chunk, return "Analysis could not be generated... please try again" instead of the misleading "No transcript lines found".

## Tests
- consolidation-failure → local merge returned, and **not** cached (asserts the summary file isn't written).
- all-chunks-fail → clear message.

content.text:
Graceful failures are in, merged ([PR #14](https://github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/pull/14), `fcbb50e`).

## What changed
Analysis no longer throws away work on a failure:

- **Consolidation fails → local merge.** If the final LLM merge errors (e.g. the API timeout you saw), the controller now deterministically merges the successful per-chunk analyses (no LLM) and returns a usable summary, marked `(Combined from N partial analyses; full consolidation was unavailable.)` instead of "Error generating summary."
- **The degraded result isn't cached** — since the failure is usually transient, the next (non-forced) load retries the real consolidation rather than locking in the cruder summary. (This was the review's main catch, and there's a test asserting the fallback isn't persisted.)
- **Hardened the recursion**: consolidation errors propagate so the fallback fires; the char-length heuristic is used only when token *estimation* fails; and a `size <= 1` guard removes a latent infinite-recursion case.
- **Clearer total-failure message**: if the model fails for every chunk, you get "Analysis could not be generated… please try again" rather than the misleading "No transcript lines found."

## Verification & workflow
- Tests: consolidation-failure → merged + not cached; all-chunks-fail → clear message.
- **162 tests** (99 backend + 44 frontend + 19 e2e), code-reviewed, CI green on all three jobs → squash-merged on green, branch pruned.

Combined with the earlier timeout+retries fix, the Gemini path is now robust end-to-end: it retries transient hiccups, and if consolidation still can't complete, you get a partial summary instead of a wall. Pull `main` and you're set.

input.new_string:
# Agent Brain Visualizer

## What is this project? The Agent Brain Visualizer is a dedicated companion tool for developers working with AI coding agents. Agents construct complex reasoning chains, dispatch background tasks, spawn subagents, and execute system commands over long-running sessions, recording all of these interactions in detailed JSONL transcript files (the agent's "brain"). It reads sessions from Antigravity (CLI/IDE/Agent), OpenAI Codex, and Claude Code. This visualizer parses those raw JSONL brain transcripts and renders them in a scannable and interactive web interface, allowing developers to inspect the agent's exact decision-making process.

content.text:
Done — fresh screenshot generated and merged ([PR #16](https://github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/pull/16), `845c223`).

I captured it by booting the app against the **synthetic e2e fixtures** (no real session content — safe to publish), then screenshotting at 2× for crispness. It shows the rebrand end-to-end: the new **Agent Brain Visualizer** logo in the sidebar, the source dropdown, the AI analysis panel (summary / flow / agent actions / issues), the stats cards, and the transcript with a rendered file link. The temporary capture script was removed (not committed).

The rename is now fully consistent — display name, logo, and screenshot all say "Agent Brain Visualizer." Shipped via the usual flow (branch → CI green on all three jobs → squash-merge on green, branch pruned).

`main` is at `845c223` and clean. Anything else?

What is something we can do with this data? Can we do analysis and come up with ways to make the source code better? Create subagents or skills that would help direct the code? Can we basically do RL / Evals and make things better?

Great question — and the data is genuinely well-suited for it. What you have is a **labeled corpus of agent trajectories**: hundreds of sessions across three agents, each with a task (the user request), the full action sequence (tool calls + args + outputs + reasoning), errors, timings, and an outcome. The app already distills each one into structured `{flow, agentActions, issues, recommendations, summary}`. The leap is going from **per-session** to **across-session** — that's where the leverage is.

Here's how I'd think about it, ordered by value-for-effort.

## 1. Aggregate mining → a concrete improvement backlog (do this first)
Roll the per-session analysis up across the whole fleet:
- **Recurring issues**, clustered — the failure modes that keep happening.
- **Aggregated recommendations** — the analyzer already emits "missing CLI tool / skill to create / AGENTS.md advice" per session; deduped and ranked, that *is* a prioritized list of ways to improve the code, tooling, and agent guidance.
- **Tool stats**: usage frequency, per-tool error/retry rates, commands that fail repeatedly, where sessions stall (idle gaps, longest steps).
- **Outcome distribution**: success vs. issues, turns-to-done, time-to-done — by repo and by agent.

This is the natural next layer of *this app* (a "fleet insights" view), reuses the existing analyzer + normalized step schema, and directly answers "make the source better." It's the highest-ROI starting point.

## 2. Derive skills / subagents / AGENTS.md from patterns
The corpus shows what agents repeatedly do and trip over:
- **Recurring multi-step tool sequences** → candidate **skills** (codify the workflow).
- **Recurring failure→fix pairs** → **AGENTS.md guidance** (exactly like what we *manually* learned this session: "build needs JDK 25 via mise", "Ollama gemma4 needs thinking disabled", "squash-merge only"). Mining makes that automatic.
- **Sub-tasks that eat many turns** → candidate **subagents** to delegate.

This is a focused extraction prompt + clustering on top of #1.

## 3. Evals (very feasible, high leverage)
Turn the corpus into an evaluation set: each session → `(task, trajectory, outcome)`. Then:
- **Metrics**: success rate, tool-calls-to-completion, error/retry rate, human-intervention count, wall-clock.
- **LLM-as-judge** rubric scoring (followed the workflow? wrote tests? minimal diff?) — we've used this judge pattern already in our reviews.
- Use it to **A/B** prompt/model/AGENTS.md changes: "did this guidance reduce retries on task-type T?" That's a measurable improvement loop.

## 4. "RL" — honest framing
True gradient RL is mostly out of reach: these agents are hosted/closed, the logs are **off-policy**, and there's no training infra here. But the *spirit* of RL is achievable and is really what you want:
- **Reward modeling offline**: define a reward (success + efficiency − errors), rank trajectories, and mine what high-reward runs do differently from low-reward ones.
- **Eval-driven optimization loop** (the practical "RL"): evals from #3 are the reward signal; you iterate on skills/AGENTS.md/prompts and re-measure. That's RLHF-lite, no GPUs.
- Real RL would be a *different* project: a trainable model + a sandbox that runs the task and verifies a reward (e.g., "does the test suite pass?") + on-policy rollouts. Your transcripts would be SFT/preference data for it at best — valuable, but the harness is the hard part.

## Honest limits
- LLM-judge scoring is subjective — calibrate against a few hand-labeled sessions.
- Off-policy logs tell you *what happened*, not *what would happen* under a change — hence the A/B loop.
- These are **real, private** sessions (your repos), so any aggregate/eval output should stay local or be scrubbed before sharing.

My recommendation: start with **#1 (fleet insights + aggregated recommendations)** — it's the smallest thing that turns this from a viewer into an *improvement engine*, and it feeds everything else. Which direction do you want to take?

questions.question: Which direction should we build first?

options.label: Fleet insights + recommendation backlog
options.description: Aggregate analysis across all sessions: recurring issues, ranked recommendations (skills/CLI/AGENTS.md candidates), tool error rates, outcome/time stats. The highest-ROI starting point; a new view/endpoint in this app.

options.label: Skill / AGENTS.md miner
options.description: Detect recurring tool-sequence workflows and failure→fix patterns, and emit concrete skill stubs and AGENTS.md guidance derived from the corpus.

options.label: Eval harness
options.description: Turn sessions into a labeled eval set with metrics (success, turns, error rate) + LLM-judge rubric scoring, to A/B prompt/model/guidance changes over time.

options.label: Just exploring for now
options.description: Don't build yet — this was a strategy discussion; I'll keep these in mind.

package io.github.glaforge.agybrainviz;

import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable;
import java.util.List;

/**
 * Aggregate, cross-session analytics for one source (flavor): how many sessions, how often they hit
 * errors, the busiest tools, the most common failures, and a rolled-up backlog of the
 * recommendations and issues surfaced by the per-session AI analyses.
 *
 * @param sampledSessions how many sessions were actually scanned (the scan is capped for
 *     responsiveness; see {@code InsightsService})
 */
@Serdeable
public record InsightsReport(
    String flavor,
    int sessionCount,
    int sampledSessions,
    int analyzedSessions,
    int toolCallTotal,
    int sessionsWithErrors,
    int cleanSessions,
    double avgToolsPerSession,
    double avgDurationSeconds,
    List<NameCount> topTools,
    List<NameCount> topErrors,
    List<NameCount> topRecommendations,
    List<NameCount> topIssues
) {}

The correct assertion is that no `<script>` element was injected. Let me fix the test:

input.prompt:
You are a senior code reviewer. Review the STAGED git diff in /Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer (run `git diff --cached`). This adds a "Fleet Insights" feature: cross-session aggregate analytics for a transcript source.

Architecture:
- `FleetInsights` (pure): aggregates many sessions' normalized steps (+ optional cached AI analysis JSON) into an `InsightsReport` (tool frequency, error frequency, outcomes, avg tools/duration, and rolled-up recommendations/issues from cached analyses). Source-agnostic, unit-tested.
- `InsightsService` (@Singleton): gathers per-flavor sessions — Antigravity from `~/.gemini/<flavor>/brain`, other sources via the `SessionSource` registry — capped at MAX_SESSIONS=150 most-recent, then calls FleetInsights.
- `InsightsController`: `GET /api/insights?flavor=` → InsightsReport (@Serdeable records NameCount/InsightsReport).
- Frontend: `insights.js` (renderInsights/showInsights), a "📊 Insights" header button wired in app.js, rendered into the transcript container.
- Tests: FleetInsightsTest (pure), InsightsControllerTest (integration), insights.test.js (vitest), e2e insights.spec.js. 104 backend + 49 frontend + 21 e2e all green.

Focus on:
1. **Correctness** of aggregation: tool counting (steps with tool_calls arrays — Antigravity packs multiple tools per step, Codex/Claude one per step), error detection (mirrors frontend isErrorStep), outcome counts (cleanSessions = sampled - withErrors), duration (first/last created_at), recommendation/issue rollup from cached summaries. Any miscount or NPE risk on missing fields?
2. **InsightsService**: correctness of the Antigravity vs SessionSource split, the MAX_SESSIONS cap + newest-first ordering, resource handling (Files.list/walk streams), performance (reads full transcript per session — up to 150 large files; acceptable? any cheap win?), and the duplicated brain-path/listing logic vs BrainController (is the duplication a real risk or acceptable?).
3. **Robustness**: malformed transcripts/summaries, empty flavor, a flavor with no sessions, `info.get("id")` null. The serde-omits-empty-arrays behavior — does the frontend safely default missing arrays? (renderInsights uses `r.topTools || []`.)
4. **Frontend**: XSS/escaping (names go through escapeHtml), the empty-report placeholders, the button wiring, and whether showInsights handles a failed fetch.
5. **Tests**: meaningful coverage, any trivial passes or gaps (e.g., Codex/Claude source path in InsightsService isn't integration-tested; the duration/tie-ordering edge cases).
6. Bugs.

Do NOT modify files. Report MUST-FIX / SHOULD-FIX / NICE-TO-HAVE with file:line and concrete fixes. Be concise.

gh pr create --base main --head feat/fleet-insights \
  --title "feat: cross-session Fleet Insights dashboard" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What & why

The first step in turning this from a *viewer* into an *improvement engine*: a **📊 Insights** view that aggregates analytics **across all of a source's sessions**, instead of one at a time.

## What it shows
- **Overview**: total sessions, clean vs. errored outcomes, total tool calls, avg tools/session, avg duration.
- **Top tools** and **most common errors** (bar charts).
- **Recommendation backlog** + **recurring issues** — rolled up and ranked from the per-session AI analyses (the `recommendations`/`issues` the analyzer already produces). This is effectively a prioritized, data-driven list of ways to improve the code, tooling, and `AGENTS.md`.

## Design
- **`FleetInsights`** (pure, source-agnostic): aggregates many sessions' normalized steps + cached analyses → `InsightsReport`. Fully unit-tested, no I/O.
- **`InsightsService`**: gathers a flavor's sessions — Antigravity from `~/.gemini/<flavor>/brain`, other sources via the `SessionSource` registry — capped at the **150 most-recent** for responsiveness while still reporting the true total. Reads `transcript_full.jsonl` when present (matching the timeline) and skips sessions that disappear mid-scan.
- **`GET /api/insights?flavor=`** → `InsightsReport`.
- **`insights.js`** renders the dashboard into the main area; a header button loads it for the selected source.

## Tests
- `FleetInsightsTest` (aggregation, outcomes, duration edge case, empty input)
- `InsightsServiceTest` (SessionSource path + the 150 cap with true-total reporting)
- `InsightsControllerTest` (integration over a temp home)
- `insights.test.js` (render + escaping + failed fetch) and an e2e spec
- **106 backend + 49 frontend + 21 e2e**, all green.

Review applied: read `transcript_full.jsonl` for parity with the timeline, made the per-session read TOCTOU-safe, and added the SessionSource/cap test.

## Follow-ups (noted, not blocking)
- Codex/Claude error grouping is coarse (their failure text isn't normalized), so "most common errors" can look granular for those sources.
- The Antigravity brain-path/listing logic is now duplicated in a couple of places; a shared helper would be a good cleanup.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text:
#1 shipped — **Fleet Insights** is merged to `main` ([PR #17](https://github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/pull/17), `b6ecd6b`).

## What you can now do
Click **📊 Insights** and the app aggregates across *all* of the selected source's sessions:
- **Overview** — sessions, clean vs. errored outcomes, total tool calls, avg tools/session, avg duration.
- **Top tools** and **most common errors** (bar charts).
- **Recommendation backlog + recurring issues** — rolled up and ranked from the per-session AI analyses. This is the direct line from data → action: a prioritized, evidence-backed list of what to fix in the code, tooling, and `AGENTS.md`.

## How it's built
A pure, source-agnostic `FleetInsights` aggregator + an `InsightsService` that gathers a flavor's sessions (Antigravity from `~/.gemini`, Codex/Claude via the `SessionSource` registry), capped at the 150 most-recent for responsiveness but reporting the true total. New `GET /api/insights` endpoint + an `insights.js` dashboard.

**Workflow followed:** branch → tests-first/verify → code review (applied: read `transcript_full.jsonl` for timeline parity, made the read TOCTOU-safe, added the SessionSource + cap test) → PR → CI green on all three jobs → squash-merge on green, branch pruned. **176 tests** now (106 backend / 49 frontend / 21 e2e).

To see it on your real data: `unset AI_PROVIDER && GEMINI_API_KEY=... mise exec -- ./gradlew run`, pick a source, hit Insights. The recommendation backlog is richest for sources where you've run AI analysis on more sessions (it aggregates those `recommendations`/`issues`).

Two honest follow-ups noted in the PR: Codex/Claude error text isn't normalized yet (so their "most common errors" can look granular), and the Antigravity brain-path logic is now duplicated in a few spots and worth extracting.

Where to next — keep mining (a "skill/AGENTS.md miner" on top of this backlog, #2), or the eval harness (#3)?
Ok which should we do next?

My pick: **#2 — the Skill / AGENTS.md miner.** Here's the reasoning.

The natural arc is **observe → act → measure**. We just built #1 (observe: the fleet dashboard + recommendation backlog). #2 is the **act** step — it turns those observations into *artifacts you can actually use*. #3 (evals) is the **measure** step, but measurement only pays off once there's a change to evaluate; running it now would just baseline the status quo.

#2 is also the better-sequenced build:
- **It compounds on what's already there.** We already aggregate recommendations/issues and tool-sequence data. #2 layers an extraction/clustering pass on top to emit concrete outputs.
- **It produces immediately-usable deliverables**: candidate **skill stubs** (codified recurring tool-sequence workflows), **AGENTS.md guidance** (recurring failure→fix lessons — exactly the kind of thing we hand-learned this session: "build needs JDK 25 via mise," "disable Ollama thinking," "squash-merge only"), and "missing tool / friction" notes. You can paste these straight into a repo.
- **It then feeds #3.** Once #2 emits guidance, the eval harness has something concrete to A/B — so doing #2 first makes #3 more valuable when we get there.

What I'd build (sketch):
- Backend: a `Patterns`/`Miner` service that, per source, finds recurring tool-sequence n-grams and failure→resolution pairs, then runs one LLM "extraction" pass to propose: skills, AGENTS.md rules, and tooling gaps — returned as structured candidates.
- Frontend: a "Mine improvements" panel (or a tab on the Insights view) listing the candidates, each copy-pasteable, with the evidence (which sessions/how often) behind it.

One honest caveat: the richness depends on the corpus, and the LLM-extraction quality. The recommendation backlog is only populated for sessions you've already AI-analyzed — so #2 will either lean on that, or do its own analysis pass over more sessions (slower, needs a provider). I'd scope v1 to mine the **structural** patterns (tool sequences, failure clusters — deterministic, no LLM) plus a single LLM pass to phrase them as skills/AGENTS.md rules.

Want me to start on #2 with that scope? If you'd rather measure before adding more, I'll flip to #3 instead.
Yes start on #2

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user owns "agent-brain-visualizer" (a Java 25/Micronaut backend + vanilla-JS frontend that visualizes AI agent session transcripts) and made a long series of requests, each shipped via a strict workflow. In chronological order of intent:
   - Build a comprehensive unit + integration + E2E test suite covering every critical user journey "so we can start making changes."
   - Add Playwright E2E and a CI workflow running all tests on PRs.
   - Make the project squash-merge-only, and grant standing authorization to squash-merge automatically once CI is green (no per-PR confirmation needed).
   - Add a local-model AI provider (Ollama/gemma4) alongside remote Gemini; default Ollama model to `gemma4`.
   - Add OpenAI Codex and Claude Code sessions as new transcript sources to visualize (with AI analysis).
   - Fix performance/reliability of analysis (Ollama "thinking" hang; Gemini timeouts; oversized Claude analysis input; graceful degradation on consolidation failure).
   - Rename the app to "Agent Brain Visualizer", update the logo image, and refresh the README screenshot.
   - Explore what can be done with the transcript corpus (analysis to improve code, derive skills/subagents/AGENTS.md, evals, RL). The user chose to "start with #1" = a cross-session Fleet Insights/analytics feature.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Project workflow: branch (feat/fix/chore/docs/refactor) → tests-first → verify (`./gradlew build`, `npm test`, Playwright) → code-review subagent → PR via `gh` → CI green → **squash-merge on green without asking** → cleanup (`git checkout main && git pull --ff-only`, prune branch). One PR per logical unit; every behavior change ships with a test.
   - Java 25 required; managed via `mise` (`mise.toml` pins `temurin-25`). Run gradle as `mise exec -- ./gradlew ...`.
   - Micronaut 5.0.2, LangChain4j 1.16.1 (google-genai + ollama), Spotless (prettier-java + js), GraalVM native (release only). Shadow jar `build/libs/*-all.jar`.
   - Frontend tests: Vitest + jsdom (`src/test/js/**`), `e2e/` Playwright against booted jar with seeded fixtures (`build/e2e-home`, `-Duser.home=...`, `GEMINI_API_KEY=dummy`), vendored `e2e/vendor/marked.min.js`.
   - AI providers via `AiConfig` (env: `AI_PROVIDER`=gemini|ollama, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_MODEL` default gemini-3.5-flash, `OLLAMA_BASE_URL`, `OLLAMA_MODEL` default gemma4). Ollama uses `.think(false).returnThinking(false)`; Gemini `.maxRetries(2).timeout(3min)`.
   - `SessionSource` registry interface (handles/listConversations/transcriptJson/sessionExists/analysisSequences/cachedSummary/deleteCache/writeCache). Implementers: `CodexSessionReader` (`~/.codex/sessions`), `ClaudeCodeSessionReader` (`~/.claude/projects`). Antigravity stays inline (`~/.gemini/<flavor>/brain`). Shared `NormalizedSteps` (analysis-sequence builder) and `SummaryCache` (traversal-guarded cache).
   - Normalized step schema: `{type, source, content, created_at, tool_calls:[{name,args}], thinking, status}`. Adapters map source-specific JSONL into it.
   - Analysis pipeline: parse → chunk (`splitIntoSafeChunks`) → parallel `analyze` → `recursivelyConsolidate` → `localMerge` fallback (not cached) on consolidation failure.
   - Repo configured squash-only + auto-merge enabled; branch protection deferred. Commits SSH-signed via 1Password.
   - Memory at `~/.claude/projects/-Users-scottdensmore-Developer-scottdensmore-agent-brain-visualizer/memory/` (MEMORY.md + squash-merge-prs.md).

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`mise.toml`** (root): pins `java = "temurin-25"` so `./gradlew` uses JDK 25.
   - **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`**: three jobs (Backend JUnit+Spotless, Frontend Vitest, E2E Playwright). Uses `actions/checkout@v7`, `setup-java@v5` (cache gradle), `setup-node@v6`, `upload-artifact@v7`. E2E build step sets `env: GITHUB_REF_NAME: e2e` (PR refs like "1/merge" otherwise nest the jar in a subdir).
   - **`AiConfig.java`**: provider selection bean with package-private test constructor; `DEFAULT_OLLAMA_MODEL = "gemma4"`.
   - **`ChatModelFactory.java`**: builds OllamaChatModel (think(false)) or GoogleGenAiChatModel (maxRetries(2), timeout 3min) based on `aiConfig.provider()`.
   - **`SessionSource.java`, `SummaryCache.java`, `NormalizedSteps.java`, `CodexAdapter.java`, `CodexSessionReader.java`, `ClaudeCodeAdapter.java`, `ClaudeCodeSessionReader.java`**: the multi-source architecture.
   - **`NormalizedSteps.java`**: `toAnalysisSequences` keeps user requests, agent tool calls, failed outputs, the FINAL assistant message (≤2000 chars), and a small budget of earlier assistant messages (`ASSISTANT_OTHER_BUDGET=2000`, `ASSISTANT_OTHER_MAX_EACH=500`); drops mid-session narration.
   - **`AnalysisController.java`**: flavor-aware summarize; consolidation failure → `localMerge(seqResponses)` (not cached, `consolidationFellBack` flag skips cache write); clear "Analysis could not be generated... please try again" when all chunks fail.
   - **Most recent — Fleet Insights (PR #17, merged b6ecd6b):**
     - `FleetInsights.java` (pure): `aggregate(String flavor, int totalSessionCount, List<Session> sessions)`; nested `record Session(List<JsonNode> steps, JsonNode cachedSummary)`. Counts tool_calls (per tool in array), errors via `isError` (status==ERROR || type==ERROR_MESSAGE || RUN_COMMAND+"The command failed"), durations (min/max created_at, zero-span excluded), rolls up recommendations/issues from cached summaries; `top()` returns top-10 `NameCount`.
     - `NameCount.java`: `@Serdeable record NameCount(String name, int count)`.
     - `InsightsReport.java`: `@Serdeable record` with flavor, sessionCount, sampledSessions, analyzedSessions, toolCallTotal, sessionsWithErrors, cleanSessions, avgToolsPerSession, avgDurationSeconds, topTools, topErrors, topRecommendations, topIssues.
     - `InsightsService.java`: `@Singleton`, injects `List<SessionSource>`; `forFlavor` → `fromSource` (registry) or `fromAntigravity`; `MAX_SESSIONS=150` (newest-first, reports true total). Review fixes applied: `readableTranscript` prefers `transcript_full.jsonl` (matching BrainController.getTranscript) while listing/sorting by `transcript.jsonl`; per-session read wrapped in try/catch (TOCTOU-safe) with null guard.
     - `InsightsController.java`: `GET /api/insights?flavor=` → `InsightsReport`, `@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.IO)`.
     - `modules/insights.js`: `renderInsights(report, container)` (stat cards, bar charts, backlog; `escapeHtml`; defaults missing arrays to `[]`) and `showInsights(flavor)` (fetch + render, error handling).
     - `index.html`: `<button id="insights-btn" class="btn" ...>📊 Insights</button>` in header-top.
     - `app.js`: `import { showInsights }`; wires `insights-btn` click to `showInsights(flavor-select.value)`.
     - Tests: `FleetInsightsTest` (3), `InsightsControllerTest` (2), `InsightsServiceTest` (2, fake SessionSource + 150-cap), `insights.test.js` (5).

4. Errors and fixes:
   - JDK 21 couldn't load Micronaut 5 Gradle plugins → installed temurin-25 via mise, added `mise.toml`.
   - CI failed (webServer couldn't start): PR `GITHUB_REF_NAME` = "1/merge" nested the jar in a subdir → used `find build/libs -name "*-all.jar"` + pinned clean `GITHUB_REF_NAME` in CI; added HTML reporter for the artifact.
   - Codex/Claude analysis "never finishing": diagnosed via direct Ollama probes that gemma4 is a thinking model (think=true ~13s vs think=false ~1.5s for trivial prompts) → `.think(false)`.
   - Claude analysis "way too long": measured 5014-line session = single ~100k-token chunk dominated by 142KB assistant text → dropped assistant narration in NormalizedSteps; then user asked to recover fidelity → kept final assistant message + budget.
   - Gemini `GenAiIOException: timeout` on consolidation → maxRetries 2 + 3min timeout; then "graceful failures" → localMerge fallback (not cached).
   - InsightsControllerTest NPE: serde omits empty arrays from JSON → made test tolerant (`topTools == null || topTools.isEmpty()`); frontend defaults missing arrays to `[]`.
   - insights.test.js escape test false positive: jsdom serializes the `title="..."` attribute with raw `<>` on read-back → changed assertion to `expect(c.querySelector("script")).toBeNull()`.
   - Compile errors fixed: missing `import java.util.Optional` (CodexSessionReader), missing `import static ...assertFalse` (AnalysisControllerTest).
   - 1Password SSH signing failed twice ("failed to fill whole buffer"); user unlocked 1Password and I retried successfully (asked via AskUserQuestion: "Unlock 1Password, I retry" vs "Commit unsigned").
   - User feedback: chose "New transcript source to visualize" for Codex; chose to keep file preview Antigravity-only (Codex/Claude have no structured file refs); chose to recover assistant-final-message fidelity; chose "start with #1" (Fleet Insights).

5. Problem Solving:
   Built a 176-test safety net (106 backend / 49 frontend / 21 e2e). Diagnosed multiple LLM/perf issues empirically (Ollama probes, chunk-count logging, analysis-input composition measurement) rather than guessing. Established a clean multi-source architecture (SessionSource registry) that made Fleet Insights and adding sources trivial. All 17 PRs merged to main on green CI.

6. All user messages:
   - "Make sure that there are plenty of unit tests and integration test that test every critical user journey in this project so we can start making changes"
   - "Are all critical user journeys tested?"
   - "How are you running all the UI tests?"
   - "Should we add playwrite?"
   - "Lets add all the tests to CI"
   - "Lets take care of that minor issue right now"
   - "how do I run this locally" / "What is the workflow for this project?"
   - "We should ensure the pr is setup to squash and merge"
   - "Lets change the workflow so it always does squash merge"
   - "Make a change to the workflow so when the PR checks goes green you can do the squash merge"
   - "We can do that later"
   - "Ok lets merge #1 first"
   - "Now that we have a good test suite: We should add the ability to use a local gemma4 model through Ollama instead of callling out to a remote gemini model with a key. Both should be supported."
   - "You can default to gemma4 here is the info https://ollama.com/library/gemma4"
   - "lets add support for OpenAI codex to the available visualizers"
   - (pasted Netty warning `onNext before request?`) / "Ok lets leave it."
   - "We should fix those limitations"
   - "how do I run this with the gemini key again?"
   - (pasted `GenAiIOException: ... InterruptedIOException: timeout`)
   - "Yeah lets add graceful failures"
   - "Lets rename this app to agent brain visualizer and update the image"
   - "Go ahead an generate one"
   - "What is something we can do with this data? Can we do analysis and come up with ways to make the source code better? Create subagents or skills that would help direct the code? Can we basically do RL / Evals and make things better?"
   - "Continue from where you left off."
   - "Ok lets start with #1"
   - Global constraint (CLAUDE.md): "Always use the GitHub CLI (`gh`) for all GitHub operations... Never use the web UI or raw API calls."

7. Pending Tasks:
   - None explicitly outstanding. The user chose "#1" (Fleet Insights), which is complete and merged (PR #17). The earlier ideation listed potential future directions #2 (skill/AGENTS.md miner), #3 (eval harness), #4 (RL/eval-driven loop) — but only #1 was authorized.

8. Current Work:
   Just completed and merged **PR #17: "feat: cross-session Fleet Insights dashboard"** (commit `b6ecd6b`), the implementation of direction "#1" from the data-analysis ideation. This adds a "📊 Insights" button that calls `GET /api/insights?flavor=` and renders a cross-session dashboard (session/outcome counts, top tools, common errors, avg tools/duration, and a recommendation/issue backlog rolled up from cached AI analyses). After a code-review subagent flagged two should-fix items, I applied: reading `transcript_full.jsonl` for timeline parity, TOCTOU-safe per-session reads, removed an unused `Comparator` import, added a duration comment, and added `InsightsServiceTest` (fake SessionSource + the MAX_SESSIONS=150 cap). Final verification: 106 backend + 49 frontend + 21 e2e all green; CI green on all three jobs; squash-merged on green; branch pruned; main synced. The last bash output showed: `git log --oneline -2` → `b6ecd6b feat: cross-session Fleet Insights dashboard (#17)` / `845c223 docs: refresh README screenshot for the renamed app (#16)`. No closing wrap-up message had yet been sent to the user when the summary was requested.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Report to the user that Fleet Insights (#1) is complete and merged (PR #17, `b6ecd6b`) — describe the new "📊 Insights" dashboard and the `/api/insights` endpoint — and ask whether to proceed to one of the other directions from the ideation (#2 skill/AGENTS.md miner, #3 eval harness, #4 RL/eval-driven loop) or stop, since the user only explicitly authorized "start with #1." This is directly in line with the user's most recent request: "Ok lets start with #1" (now done). I should NOT begin #2/#3/#4 without explicit confirmation, consistent with the original ideation where I had offered those as distinct directions via AskUserQuestion.

14	 * limitations under the License.
15	 */
16	package io.github.glaforge.agybrainviz;
17	
18	import dev.langchain4j.model.chat.ChatModel;
19	import dev.langchain4j.model.chat.request.ResponseFormat;
20	import dev.langchain4j.model.google.genai.GoogleGenAiChatModel;
21	import dev.langchain4j.model.ollama.OllamaChatModel;
22	import io.micronaut.context.annotation.Factory;
23	import jakarta.inject.Inject;
24	import jakarta.inject.Singleton;
25	import java.time.Duration;
26	
27	/**
28	 * Produces the {@link ChatModel} used by {@link AnalyzerService}, choosing between the remote Google
29	 * Gemini API and a local Ollama model based on {@link AiConfig#provider()}.
30	 */
31	@Factory
32	public class ChatModelFactory {
33	
34	    private final AiConfig aiConfig;
35	
36	    @Inject
37	    public ChatModelFactory(AiConfig aiConfig) {
38	        this.aiConfig = aiConfig;
39	    }
40	
41	    @Singleton
42	    public ChatModel chatModel() {
43	        if (aiConfig.provider() == AiConfig.Provider.OLLAMA) {
44	            return OllamaChatModel
45	                .builder()
46	                .baseUrl(aiConfig.ollamaBaseUrl())
47	                .modelName(aiConfig.ollamaModel())
48	                .temperature(0.0)
49	                // Disable "thinking": for reasoning-capable local models (e.g. Gemma) Ollama would
50	                // otherwise generate a long hidden chain-of-thought on every analysis call, which
51	                // can take minutes per request and makes the analysis appear to hang. We only need
52	                // the structured summary, not the reasoning.
53	                .think(false)
54	                .returnThinking(false)
55	                // Local models can be slower than the hosted API, so allow more time per request.
56	                .timeout(Duration.ofMinutes(5))
57	                .responseFormat(ResponseFormat.JSON)
58	                .build();
59	        }
60	
61	        return GoogleGenAiChatModel
62	            .builder()
63	            .apiKey(aiConfig.geminiApiKey().orElse("dummy"))
64	            .modelName(aiConfig.geminiModel())
65	            .temperature(0.0)
66	            // Retry transient API/network failures (e.g. HTTP timeouts on the large consolidation
67	            // request) instead of failing the whole analysis after the per-chunk work succeeded.
68	            .maxRetries(2)
69	            .timeout(Duration.ofMinutes(3))
70	            .responseFormat(ResponseFormat.JSON)
71	            .build();
72	    }
73	}
74

20	import dev.langchain4j.service.V;
21	import io.micronaut.langchain4j.annotation.AiService;
22	
23	@AiService
24	public interface AnalyzerService {
25	    @SystemMessage("""
26				You are an expert at analyzing JSONL transcripts of Antigravity CLI sessions.
27				Your job is to extract the core insights, actions, issues, and actionable recommendations
28				(e.g. missing CLI tools, helpful skills to create, or AGENTS.md advice) into a structured format.
29				""")
30	    @UserMessage("""
31				Please analyze the following JSONL transcript of an Antigravity CLI session.
32	
33				CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS:
34				- NEVER fall into an infinite repetition loop. Do NOT repeat the exact same phrase or word over and over.
35				- Be succinct and concise in your lists. Keep the overall size of the output compact.
36				- Keep description texts short. Each issue or action description MUST be 1 or 2 sentences maximum.
37				- DO NOT output Base64. DO NOT output infinitely repeating words or phrases. If you find yourself repeating, STOP immediately.
38				- Your summary MUST BE SHORT. No more than 3 paragraphs. Do NOT embed raw logs, code, or large quotes in the summary.
39				- Output MUST be exclusively in English. No other languages are permitted.
40	
41				Transcript:
42				{{transcript}}
43				""")
44	    AnalysisResponse analyze(@V("transcript") String transcript);
45	
46	    @UserMessage("""
47				Here is the structured analysis from the previous parts of the conversation:
48	
49				{{previousAnalysis}}
50	
51				Please update and enhance this analysis using the next section of the transcript below.

content.text:
I have everything I need. This is the **skill / AGENTS.md miner** (#2). Plan: a pure `PatternMiner` (deterministic tool-sequence n-grams + failure→fix pairs + recommendation rollup), a shared `SessionCollector` (extracted from `InsightsService` to kill the duplication the #17 review flagged), one LLM pass (`MinerAdvisorService`) that phrases the evidence into skills + AGENTS.md rules, a `/api/mine` endpoint, and a "🛠️ Mine" panel — degrading gracefully to just the evidence when AI isn't configured.

Let me branch and start tests-first.

package io.github.glaforge.agybrainviz;

import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable;
import java.util.List;

/**
 * The output of the "skill / AGENTS.md miner" for one source: the structural evidence mined across
 * sessions plus, when AI is configured, the LLM-proposed skills, AGENTS.md rules, and tooling gaps.
 *
 * @param aiGenerated whether the LLM phrasing pass ran; when false, only the evidence is populated
 * @param note a short human-facing explanation when {@code aiGenerated} is false (e.g. AI not set up)
 */
@Serdeable
public record MiningReport(
    String flavor,
    int sessionCount,
    int sampledSessions,
    int analyzedSessions,
    boolean aiGenerated,
    String note,
    List<NameCount> toolSequences,
    List<FixPair> failureFixes,
    List<NameCount> recommendations,
    List<SkillProposal> skills,
    List<AgentsRule> agentsRules,
    List<String> toolingGaps
) {}

input.prompt:
Review the staged git diff on the current branch `feat/skill-agents-miner` in the repo at /Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer. Run `git diff --cached` to see it.

This adds a "skill / AGENTS.md miner" feature to a Java 25 / Micronaut backend + vanilla-JS frontend that visualizes AI agent session transcripts. Key new pieces:
- `PatternMiner.java` (pure): deterministically mines recurring tool-call n-grams (2/3-grams counted per-session), failure→fix pairs and recommendations from sessions' normalized steps + cached AI summaries.
- `SessionCollector.java`: extracted from `InsightsService` (which is now slimmed to use it) to gather a flavor's sessions; Antigravity from ~/.gemini, others via SessionSource registry; capped at MAX_SESSIONS=150.
- `MinerService.java`: gathers → mines → (if AI configured) runs one `MinerAdvisorService` (@AiService) LLM pass to phrase evidence into skills/AGENTS.md rules/tooling gaps. Degrades gracefully (returns evidence only, aiGenerated=false + note) when no evidence, AI unconfigured, or the LLM call throws.
- `MineController.java`: GET /api/mine?flavor=.
- Records: `FixPair`, `SkillProposal`, `AgentsRule`, `MiningProposal` (LLM output), `MiningReport` (endpoint output).
- Frontend `modules/mine.js` (`renderMining`/`showMining`) + a "🛠️ Mine" button wired in app.js/index.html.
- Tests: PatternMinerTest, MinerServiceTest, MineControllerTest, mine.test.js, e2e/mine.spec.js.

Focus your review on: correctness bugs, the SessionCollector extraction not changing InsightsService behavior, null/empty-safety, XSS/escaping in mine.js (it builds innerHTML — verify all dynamic values go through escapeHtml), thread-safety/resource leaks, and any should-fix issues. The whole project follows: every behavior change ships with a test, graceful degradation over erroring, source-agnostic pure aggregators.

Do NOT modify files. Return a concise list of findings categorized as MUST-FIX / SHOULD-FIX / NITPICK, each with file:line and a one-line fix. If you find nothing material, say so.

<result>The e2e mirrors insights.spec.js which already asserts "Add a lint rule" against seeded fixtures, so the seeding exists. 

One thing to double check on the e2e: the second test selects `claude-code` and expects "Claude Code" text. But if claude-code has no sessions seeded, the report renders with label "Claude Code" in the subtitle regardless — so the assertion holds even with zero evidence. Good.

Let me verify the `recommendations` vs `failureFixes` per-session-counting consistency concern is real by re-reading the relevant PatternMiner lines, and check the `analyzed` counter isn't double counted. The mine loop: for each session, sequences are added (deduped per session via Set), then summary issues/recs are iterated. Recommendations use `recFreq.merge(text, 1, ...)` per occurrence — so if one session lists the same recommendation twice, it counts 2. Same for fixes. This differs from tool sequences (per-session). This is a minor semantic inconsistency, not a bug. I have enough to report.

## Review: skill / AGENTS.md miner

I reviewed the full staged diff (all 20 files): the three core classes (`PatternMiner`, `SessionCollector`, `MinerService`), the `InsightsService` extraction, controller/advisor/records, `mine.js`, and all five test files. I also cross-checked `escapeHtml`, `AiConfig.isConfigured`, `NameCount`, and the existing `AnalyzerService`/`AnalysisController` DI pattern.

### MUST-FIX
None. No correctness, safety, thread-safety, or resource-leak bugs found.

### SHOULD-FIX
None material. The behaviors the prompt flagged as risks all check out (details below).

### NITPICK
- `PatternMiner.java:78,84` — failure→fix pairs and recommendations are counted **per occurrence** (`merge(..., 1, ...)` / `FixAccumulator.add` per issue), whereas tool sequences are deduped **per session** (via the `Set` in `sequencesIn`). So a single session that lists the same error/recommendation twice contributes a count of 2, which the UI renders as "×2 sessions" — slightly overstating session breadth. Tests don't exercise this case. Optional: dedupe issues/recs per session for consistency with the sequence semantics. Not a bug, just a labeling nuance.
- `mine.js:182` (`showMining`) — `res.ok` is not checked before `res.json()`. A 5xx with a JSON error body would flow into `renderMining`, which tolerates it via the `report || {}` guard and renders an empty report rather than the "Failed to mine improvements" message. Harmless (degrades gracefully), but checking `res.ok` would surface server errors more clearly.
- `MinerService.java:67` / `SessionCollector.java` — uses `System.err.println` for the advisor-failure log, consistent with the existing codebase style (`AnalyzerService` path uses the same), so fine; a logger would be marginally nicer.

### Verified clean (the prompt's focus areas)
- **SessionCollector extraction is behavior-preserving.** The `fromSource`/`fromAntigravity`/parse/`nonEmpty`/`lastModified` logic is moved verbatim; `InsightsService.forFlavor` now just calls `collector.collect` then `FleetInsights.aggregate(flavor, totalSessionCount, sessions)` — identical arguments to before. `MAX_SESSIONS` moved to `SessionCollector` and `InsightsServiceTest` updated accordingly. The `flavor`-null/empty default (`"antigravity-cli"`) is preserved.
- **XSS/escaping in mine.js.** Every dynamic string value flows through `escapeHtml`: `title`, `label`, `note`, `skill.name/whenToUse/body`, `rule.rule/rationale`, `pair.error`, `fix`, `seq.name`, `rec.name`, and `toolingGaps` entries. The only unescaped interpolations are numeric `count`/`sessionCount`/`analyzedSessions` fields, which are `int` on the server records (`NameCount`, `FixPair`, `MiningReport`) — not attacker-controllable as markup. `mine.test.js` includes an explicit `<script>` injection assertion. Confirmed safe.
- **Null/empty-safety.** `MinerService.orEmpty` guards null LLM lists; `proposal == null` is handled; `renderMining` guards `report || {}` and `|| []` on every list; `PatternMiner` skips null/missing/blank summary nodes and `clean()` null-guards + truncates to 200 chars. Empty-session and no-evidence paths are tested.
- **Graceful degradation.** Three degrade paths (no evidence / AI unconfigured / advisor throws) all return evidence with `aiGenerated=false` + a note; `MinerAdvisorService` is injected unconditionally exactly like `AnalyzerService`, and calls are gated on `aiConfig.isConfigured()`, so DI won't fail when AI is unconfigured. All three paths have unit tests plus an integration test (`MineControllerTest`) that exercises the no-key path end-to-end.
- **Thread-safety/resource leaks.** `SessionCollector` uses a static `ObjectMapper` (thread-safe for reads), the only `Files.list` stream is in try-with-resources, and `@ExecuteOn(TaskExecutors.IO)` keeps blocking I/O off the event loop. No shared mutable state in the miner.

Key files: `src/main/java/io/github/glaforge/agybrainviz/PatternMiner.java`, `SessionCollector.java`, `MinerService.java`, `src/main/resources/public/modules/mine.js`.

Net: solid, well-tested, follows the project's test-per-change / graceful-degradation / source-agnostic conventions. The two nitpicks are optional.</result>

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: skill / AGENTS.md miner from session corpus" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What

Direction #2 from the data-analysis ideation: turn the cross-session corpus into **reusable engineering assets**. A new **🛠️ Mine** button (next to 📊 Insights) calls `GET /api/mine?flavor=` and renders:

- **Proposed Skills** — codified recurring workflows (name · when-to-use · numbered body)
- **Proposed AGENTS.md Rules** — durable imperative guidelines with the evidence-based rationale
- **Tooling Gaps** — missing tools / friction the failures imply
- **Evidence** (always shown) — recurring tool-call workflows, failure→fix pairs, and the recommendation backlog the proposals were derived from

This is the **observe → act** step on top of #17's Insights: it converts the aggregated signal into copy-pasteable artifacts.

## How

- **`PatternMiner`** (pure, source-agnostic, fully unit-tested): deterministically mines consecutive tool-call 2/3-grams (candidate workflows), failure→fix pairs, and recommendations from sessions' normalized steps + cached AI summaries. Everything is counted by **session breadth** so one looping session can't masquerade as a fleet-wide pattern.
- **`SessionCollector`**: extracted from `InsightsService` (now slimmed to use it), killing the Antigravity-vs-`SessionSource` gather duplication the #17 review flagged. Shared `MAX_SESSIONS=150` cap; true total still reported.
- **`MinerService`**: gather → mine → one `MinerAdvisorService` (`@AiService`, shared `ChatModel`) pass to phrase the evidence. **Degrades gracefully** — returns evidence only with `aiGenerated=false` + an explanatory note when there's no evidence, AI isn't configured, or the model call throws (never errors).
- **`MineController`**: `GET /api/mine?flavor=`, `@ExecuteOn(IO)`.

## Tests

Every behavior change ships with a test, per project convention:
- `PatternMinerTest` (5) — n-gram recurrence, per-session counting, fix/rec rollup, empty
- `MinerServiceTest` (4) — AI-on proposals, AI-off, advisor-failure degradation, no-evidence
- `MineControllerTest` (2) — end-to-end over a temp `user.home`, exercises the no-key graceful path
- `mine.test.js` (7) — render (proposals + evidence), AI-off note, XSS escaping, empty, fetch ok/non-ok/error
- `e2e/mine.spec.js` (2) — button renders evidence; follows the selected source

Verification: `./gradlew build` (Spotless + JUnit) ✅ · 56 frontend ✅ · 23 e2e ✅

## Review

A code-review pass found **no MUST-FIX / SHOULD-FIX**. Both nitpicks applied: per-session counting for fixes/recommendations (consistency with the "×N sessions" label) and a `res.ok` check in `showMining`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
Done. **#2 — the skill / AGENTS.md miner — is merged to `main`** ([PR #18](https://github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/pull/18), `9332e35`). Branch pruned, main synced.

## What you can do now
Click **🛠️ Mine** (next to 📊 Insights). For the selected source it aggregates the session corpus and shows:
- **Proposed Skills** — recurring tool-call workflows codified into name · when-to-use · numbered steps, ready to drop into a project.
- **Proposed AGENTS.md Rules** — durable imperative guidelines (with the evidence-based *why*) distilled from the failure→fix pairs — exactly the kind of lesson we hand-learned this session ("build needs JDK 25 via mise", "disable Ollama thinking").
- **Tooling Gaps** — missing tools / friction the failures imply.
- **Evidence** (always shown) — the recurring workflows, failure→fix pairs, and recommendation backlog the proposals were derived from, so every suggestion is traceable.

If AI isn't configured (or the model call fails), it degrades to **evidence-only** with a note, never an error.

## How it's built
- `PatternMiner` — pure, source-agnostic miner of tool-call n-grams + failure→fix pairs + recommendations, counted by **session breadth**.
- `SessionCollector` — extracted from `InsightsService`, which finally **kills the gather duplication the #17 review flagged** (both features now share it).
- `MinerService` — gather → mine → one LLM phrasing pass, with three graceful-degradation paths.
- `GET /api/mine?flavor=` + the `mine.js` panel.

**Workflow followed end to end:** branch → tests-first → `./gradlew build` / 56 frontend / 23 e2e all green → code review (clean; both nitpicks applied — per-session counting + `res.ok` check) → PR → CI green on all three jobs → squash-merge → branch pruned. **+20 tests** (now 13 mine-specific across unit/integration/e2e).

That completes #1 (observe) and #2 (act). The remaining direction is **#3 — the eval harness** (measure: turn sessions into a labeled eval set so you can A/B these mined rules and prompt/model changes). It's now well-motivated, since #2 produces concrete guidance to evaluate. Want me to start on #3, or pause here?
Yes lets stat on number 3

On to **#3 — the eval harness (measure)**.

My v1 scope, consistent with how #1/#2 shipped: a **deterministic analysis-quality eval**. It grades the analyses the app has already produced (cached summaries) against their transcripts using pure, fast graders — schema validity, actionability, conciseness, non-degeneracy, and error-coverage — and rolls them into a **scoreboard** (avg score, per-check pass-rates, worst offenders) labeled with the **active provider/model**. That gives you the measurement half of the loop: change model/prompt → *Recompute Analysis* (existing feature) → re-run Eval → compare scoreboards.

I'm deliberately keeping the **LLM-judge** (rubric scoring 1–5 for faithfulness/actionability) as the immediate fast-follow (#3b) so this PR stays a clean, deterministic, fast, fully-testable unit — the deterministic graders are exactly the cheap gate a judge layers on top of. I'll flag it at the end.

package io.github.glaforge.agybrainviz;

import io.micronaut.serde.annotation.Serdeable;
import java.util.List;

/**
 * The analysis-quality scoreboard for one source: how many of its cached analyses were graded, their
 * average score, how often each named check passed, and the lowest-scoring cases to look at first.
 *
 * @param modelLabel the AI provider/model currently configured, so two runs (e.g. after switching
 *     model or prompt and recomputing analyses) can be compared apples-to-apples
 * @param checkPassRates for each named check, how many of the evaluated sessions passed it
 */
@Serdeable
public record EvalReport(
    String flavor,
    int sessionCount,
    int sampledSessions,
    int evaluatedSessions,
    double avgScore,
    String modelLabel,
    List<NameCount> checkPassRates,
    List<EvalCaseResult> worstCases
) {}

input.prompt:
Review the staged git diff on branch `feat/analysis-eval-harness` in /Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/agent-brain-visualizer. Run `git diff --cached` to see it.

This adds an "analysis quality eval harness" to a Java 25 / Micronaut backend + vanilla-JS frontend that visualizes AI agent session transcripts. New pieces:
- `EvalScorer.java` (pure): deterministically grades one session's cached AI analysis (a JsonNode) against its transcript steps into an `EvalCaseResult` (0-100 score + which named checks passed/failed). Checks: schema-complete, has-recommendations, issues-have-fixes, concise-summary, not-degenerate (token-repeat / Base64 heuristic), error-coverage.
- `EvalService.java`: gathers a flavor's sessions via the shared `SessionCollector`, scores only those with a cached analysis, aggregates into an `EvalReport` (avgScore, per-check pass-rate as List<NameCount>, bottom-10 worstCases, and a modelLabel from AiConfig for A/B context). No LLM calls — fast/deterministic.
- `EvalController.java`: GET /api/eval?flavor=.
- Records `EvalCaseResult`, `EvalReport`.
- `FleetInsights.Session` gained an `id` field with a backward-compatible 2-arg constructor; `SessionCollector` now populates the id (from SessionSource info "id", or the Antigravity dir name). Verify this change didn't alter InsightsService/MinerService/FleetInsights aggregation behavior (they ignore id).
- Frontend `modules/eval.js` (`renderEval`/`showEval`) + a "🧪 Eval" button wired in app.js/index.html.
- Tests: EvalScorerTest, EvalServiceTest, EvalControllerTest, eval.test.js, e2e/eval.spec.js.

Focus: correctness bugs in the scorer (off-by-one in score %, the degeneracy/repeat heuristic, error detection), the FleetInsights.Session id change being behavior-preserving for existing callers, null/empty-safety on missing analysis fields, XSS/escaping in eval.js (it builds innerHTML — verify every dynamic value goes through escapeHtml; note numeric fields are ints server-side), and any should-fix issues. The project conventions: every behavior change ships a test, graceful degradation over erroring, pure source-agnostic aggregators.

Do NOT modify files. Return findings as MUST-FIX / SHOULD-FIX / NITPICK with file:line and a one-line fix each. If nothing material, say so.

content.text:
The eval harness (#3 v1) is built and fully green locally — I've launched a code-review subagent on the staged diff and will apply any findings, then open the PR and merge on green.

**What's built** — a **🧪 Eval** button → `GET /api/eval?flavor=` that scores the analyses the app has already produced against their transcripts with six deterministic, no-LLM graders (schema-complete, has-recommendations, issues-have-fixes, concise-summary, not-degenerate, error-coverage) and renders a scoreboard: avg score, per-check pass-rates, and the lowest-scoring analyses — all labeled with the **active provider/model** so you can A/B (switch model → *Recompute Analysis* → re-Eval → compare).

**Verification:** backend build (Spotless + JUnit, +6 eval tests) · 62 frontend (+7) · 25 E2E (+2). The one design touch worth noting — `FleetInsights.Session` gained an `id` (with a back-compat 2-arg constructor so no existing caller/test changed); the reviewer is checking that's behavior-preserving.

I'll report back once the review completes and it's merged.

One last check: in `SessionCollector` line 72-75, `info.get("id")` could be null in theory, but that's the pre-existing id used for `transcriptJson(id)` too, so no new risk. And the Antigravity branch uses `dir.getFileName().toString()` which is non-null. Good.

The prompt flagged "the degeneracy/repeat heuristic" specifically. 6 identical tokens in a row trips it. That's a reasonable threshold; the constant name `MAX_CONSECUTIVE_REPEATS` is slightly misleading (it's "max consecutive occurrences" — trips at the 6th occurrence = 5 repeats) but not a bug. Noting as nitpick.

I have everything needed.

## Review: `feat/analysis-eval-harness` eval harness

I reviewed the full staged diff (scorer, service, controller, records, `FleetInsights.Session` change, `SessionCollector`, `eval.js`, and all tests) and cross-checked against the existing analysis schema (`AnalysisResponse`/`Issue`), transcript-step error fields (`FleetInsights.isError`), and all `Session` constructor callers.

**Nothing material / no MUST-FIX.** The core concerns you flagged all hold up:

- **Score %** — `(int) Math.round(100.0 * passed.size() / total)` is correct (3/6→50, 5/6→83); no off-by-one. `avgScore` `round1` is correct. Empty-set guard (`total==0`) present.
- **`FleetInsights.Session` id change is behavior-preserving.** The 2-arg convenience constructor delegates to `(null, steps, cachedSummary)`; the only 2-arg callers are `PatternMinerTest`/`FleetInsightsTest`, and `FleetInsights.aggregate` reads only `steps()`/`cachedSummary()`. `SessionCollector` populates id from the same `info.get("id")` already used for `transcriptJson(id)` (SessionSource branch) and the Antigravity dir name (non-null). InsightsService/MinerService untouched. Confirmed.
- **Null/empty-safety** — `EvalScorer` uses `path(...).asText("")` + `nonBlank` throughout; `EvalService` skips `null`/`isMissingNode`/`isNull` analyses and defaults id to `"unknown"`. Good graceful degradation.
- **XSS in `eval.js`** — every string field (`title`, `flavor` label, `modelLabel`, `sessionId`, check names/labels, `avgScore` via `String(...)`) goes through `escapeHtml`. The unescaped interpolations (`item.count`, `evaluated`, `pct`, `c.score`, `evaluated`) are all numeric — server-side `int`/`double` per the records — and `pct` is client-computed from them, so safe. The dedicated escaping test passes.

### SHOULD-FIX (1)
- **`EvalScorer.stepsHaveError` diverges from `FleetInsights.isError`** — `EvalScorer.java:118-127` matches only `status=="ERROR"` and `type=="ERROR_MESSAGE"`, but the canonical error detector (`FleetInsights.java:135-139`) also treats `type=="RUN_COMMAND"` with content containing "The command failed" as an error. Result: a transcript whose only failure is a failed shell command won't demand `error-coverage`, so a weak analysis passes that check vacuously. Fix: reuse/mirror `FleetInsights.isError` (add the `RUN_COMMAND` + "The command failed" clause) so the two error definitions stay in sync. Per the "every behavior change ships a test" convention, add a scorer case with a failed-command step.

### NITPICK (2)
- **`MAX_CONSECUTIVE_REPEATS = 6` is misnamed** (`EvalScorer.java:47`, used at :139). The loop trips on the 6th *occurrence* of a token (= 5 repeats after the first), not 6 repeats. Behavior is fine and tested; the name reads as "occurrences," not "repeats." One-line fix: rename to `MAX_CONSECUTIVE_OCCURRENCES` (or start `run` at 0).
- **`EvalReport.sampledSessions` is populated but unused by the frontend** (`EvalService.java:79` sets it to `collected.sessions().size()`; `eval.js` renders `sessionCount`/`evaluatedSessions` only). Harmless — kept for JSON completeness/A-B context — but if it's meant to be surfaced, `eval.js:renderEval` doesn't show it. Leave as-is or drop it.

No changes made to any files, as requested.</result>

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: analysis-quality eval harness" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What

Direction #3 (v1) from the data-analysis ideation: the **measure** step. A new **🧪 Eval** button (next to 🛠️ Mine / 📊 Insights) calls `GET /api/eval?flavor=` and scores how good the AI analyses the app has already produced actually are — rendering a scoreboard:

- **Avg score** (0–100) and **evaluated / total** sessions
- **Per-check pass-rates** across the corpus
- **Lowest-scoring analyses** (with which checks they failed), so you know what to look at first
- Labelled with the **active provider/model**, so two runs compare apples-to-apples

This closes the loop on #1 (observe) → #2 (act) → **#3 (measure)**: change the model or a prompt → **Recompute Analysis** (existing feature) → re-run Eval → compare scoreboards.

## How

- **`EvalScorer`** (pure, source-agnostic, fully unit-tested): grades one session's cached analysis against its transcript into a 0–100 score across six deterministic checks — `schema-complete`, `has-recommendations`, `issues-have-fixes`, `concise-summary`, `not-degenerate` (token-repeat / Base64 heuristic — the exact failure mode the pipeline's prompts guard against), `error-coverage`. **No LLM calls** → fast, deterministic, cheap to run repeatedly.
- **`EvalService`**: gathers a flavor's sessions via the shared `SessionCollector`, scores only those with a cached analysis, aggregates into an `EvalReport` (avg, per-check pass-rate, bottom-10 worst cases, model label).
- **`EvalController`**: `GET /api/eval?flavor=`, `@ExecuteOn(IO)`.
- `FleetInsights.Session` gained an `id` (via a **back-compat 2-arg constructor**, so no existing aggregation caller/test changed); `SessionCollector` populates it so the eval can name its worst cases.

## Tests

Per project convention, every behavior change ships with a test:
- `EvalScorerTest` (7) — perfect/empty/degenerate/oversized/missing-fix/failed-command-coverage/check-completeness
- `EvalServiceTest` (2) — scores only cached analyses + aggregates; zero when nothing analyzed
- `EvalControllerTest` (2) — end-to-end over a temp `user.home`
- `eval.test.js` (6) — scoreboard render, empty-state, XSS escaping, fetch ok/non-ok/error
- `e2e/eval.spec.js` (2) — button scores the source; follows the selected source

Verification: `./gradlew build` (Spotless + JUnit) ✅ · 62 frontend ✅ · 25 e2e ✅

## Review

A code-review pass found **no MUST-FIX**. Applied the one SHOULD-FIX — unified error detection so `EvalScorer` reuses the canonical `FleetInsights.isError` (a failed `RUN_COMMAND` now also demands `error-coverage`), with a new test — plus a constant rename.

## Next (#3b)

This is the deterministic backbone; the **LLM-judge** dimension (rubric scores 1–5 for faithfulness/actionability, opt-in + capped, graceful-degrading like the miner) layers on top as the fast-follow.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

Done. **#3 — the analysis-quality eval harness — is merged to `main`** ([PR #19](https://github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/pull/19), `4459a24`). Branch pruned, main synced.

## What you can do now
Click **🧪 Eval**. For the selected source it grades every analysis the app has already produced against its transcript and shows:
- **Avg score** (0–100) and how many of the sessions were evaluated.
- **Per-check pass-rates** across six deterministic checks — schema completeness, has-recommendations, issues-have-fixes, concise-summary, not-degenerate (catches the token-repeat/Base64 failure mode), and error-coverage.
- **Lowest-scoring analyses** with the exact checks they failed — your triage list.
- The **active provider/model** it was scored under.

**The A/B loop is now closed:** switch `AI_PROVIDER`/model → *Recompute Analysis* → re-run Eval → compare scoreboards. No LLM calls in the eval itself, so it's fast and free to run as often as you like.

## The arc, complete
- **#1 Insights** (observe) → **#2 Miner** (act: skills + AGENTS.md rules) → **#3 Eval** (measure). Each shipped as one clean PR through the full workflow: tests-first → build/62 frontend/25 e2e green → code review (no MUST-FIX; SHOULD-FIX applied — unified error detection with `FleetInsights.isError`) → CI green → squash-merge.

Three follow-ups are teed up, in rough priority:
1. **#3b — LLM-judge dimension**: rubric scores 1–5 for faithfulness/actionability layered on the deterministic backbone (opt-in, capped, graceful-degrading like the miner). The natural next increment.
2. **Normalize Codex/Claude error text** (carried over from #17) so their "common errors" aren't over-granular.
3. **Persist eval runs** so scoreboards can be diffed over time instead of compared by eye.

Want me to take **#3b** next, or stop here?
Yes, take #3b next
